title: Server tests — doctor cite-coverage end-to-end (events.jsonl fixtures, denominator, metrics, per-client, histogram)
description: Comprehensive service-layer test suite for runDoctorCiteCoverage covering: empty ledger (skipped), single-turn happy path, multi-cite per turn, narrow vs broad denominator, recalled verified vs unverified (session correlation), expected_but_missed detection, dismissed reason histogram, per-client split, --since window filter, --client filter, large-ledger performance (10k events <100ms).

files.change: Add comprehensive test block using existing writeFile / createInitializedProject / FABRIC_HOME isolation patterns. ~12 test cases: (1) empty ledger → status:'skipped'; (2) marker present + no turns → metrics all 0, status:'ok'; (3) single planned cite + 1 matching edit → qualifying_cites=1, edits_touched=1; (4) narrow KB with relevance_paths=['src/foo/**'] + edit on src/foo/bar.ts → denominator counts that edit; (5) narrow KB + edit on UNMATCHED path → denominator=0 for that cite; (6) broad KB (empty relevance_paths) + 3 edits → denominator=3 for that cite; (7) recalled tag + matching fab_get_knowledge_sections in same session → not in recalled_unverified; (8) recalled tag + NO matching fetch → recalled_unverified++; (9) dismissed:scope-mismatch + dismissed:outdated + dismissed:other:foo → histogram has 3 buckets ({scope-mismatch:1, outdated:1, other:1}); (10) per-client: 2 turns from cc + 1 from codex → per_client.cc.total_turns=2, per_client.codex.total_turns=1; (11) --since=1h filters out events older than 1h ago; (12) --client=cc filters out codex turns; (13) expected_but_missed: edit on path matching KB.relevance_paths but no cite emitted in same session → expected_but_missed=1; (14) performance: seed 10k events → runDoctorCiteCoverage completes in <100ms (use performance.now()).

implementation: Read packages/server/src/services/doctor.test.ts L391-L426 + L850-L870 to confirm writeFileSync ledger-seeding pattern.
Read packages/server/src/services/doctor.test.ts L29-L47 to confirm FABRIC_HOME beforeEach isolation.
Define test helper `seedEvents(target, events)` that JSON.stringify+'\n'+writes to .fabric/events.jsonl atomically.
Define test helper `seedAgentsMeta(target, nodes)` that writes a minimal agents.meta.json with stable_id → {description: {relevance_paths, relevance_scope}} entries.
Define test helper `mkTurnEvent({sessionId, envelopeIndex, kbLineRaw, citeIds, citeTags, client, ts})` building a valid assistant_turn_observed event.
Define test helper `mkEditEvent({path, ts, sessionId?})` building a valid edit_intent_checked event.
Define test helper `mkMarkerEvent({ts})` building a valid cite_policy_activated event.
Write all 14 test cases following describe → it block pattern; each fully isolated via beforeEach.
For performance test: pre-generate 10k events via faker-ish loop; assert wall time < 100ms.
Run `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-server test doctor` and confirm all 14 new tests pass.

reference.examples: Existing event-replay tests (e.g. buildLastActiveIndex tests if present, or doctor_run telemetry tests) show seed→read→assert pattern.

rationale.chosen_approach: Fixture-based filesystem tests (no MCP runtime, no live AI) — pure-function semantics of the replay algorithm make this clean. Comprehensive enum + edge case coverage protects against silent metric drift as the algorithm matures.
alternatives_considered: Snapshot tests on rendered report (rejected: brittle to i18n key tweaks)
Integration tests via real fab CLI shell-out (rejected: slower, less granular)
Property-based testing (rejected: overkill for first cut; useful for follow-up)
decision_factors: Service-layer is the natural test surface
All inputs (events + meta) are filesystem fixtures — no mocking needed
Performance test catches regression on the locked <100ms invariant
rationale.tradeoffs: Large test file growth (~14 cases). Mitigated by helper extraction (seedEvents/mkTurnEvent/etc.) — each test stays <20 lines.

manual_checks: Run `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-server test doctor` and confirm all new tests pass
Intentionally regress runDoctorCiteCoverage (e.g. flip narrow denominator filter) to confirm tests catch it
Verify performance test does NOT flake on CI (run 3x locally)

criteria: ≥14 new test cases added to doctor.test.ts under describe('runDoctorCiteCoverage')
All 14 tests pass on `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-server test doctor`
Test helpers (seedEvents, seedAgentsMeta, mkTurnEvent, mkEditEvent, mkMarkerEvent) extracted and reused
Performance test confirms <100ms wall time for 10k seeded events
Each enum value in cite_tags (planned/recalled/chained-from/dismissed/none) covered by at least one test
Each dismissed reason enum value (scope-mismatch/outdated/not-applicable/other) covered by histogram test
Per-client split test verifies both cc and codex bucketing
Since-filter test verifies events older than window are excluded
Commit msg: `test(rc20): doctor cite-coverage service-layer tests (TASK-08)`
